name: Analysis of plant scRNA-Seq Data with Scanpy
description: This training material provides guidance on analyzing single-cell RNA sequencing (scRNA-Seq) data from plants using the Scanpy framework. It's a tutorial focused on a specific workflow and potentially a paper replication exercise. This resource aims to equip users with the skills to process and interpret scRNA-seq data in a plant biology context. It is available through the PaN-Training platform.

name: Identification and Evolutionary Analysis of Transcription-Associated Proteins in Streptophyte algae and Land plants
description: This training material provides a tutorial on the identification and evolutionary analysis of transcription-associated proteins in Streptophyte algae and land plants. It likely employs bioinformatics methods for sequence analysis and is targeted towards researchers in plant molecular biology. The material is hosted on the PaN Training platform and utilizes resources from the Galaxy Project.
teaches: Apply bioinformatics methods for sequence analysis in plant biology

The project promotes FAIR and open training, breaking down barriers between thematic communities.

These materials support research data management, FAIR data principles, and computational skills development.  Topics include bioinformatics, data management, and high-performance computing. The repository allows filtering by date added and scientific topic.

name: EOSC Academy
description: EOSC Academy is a training portal for the photon & neutron community. It provides access to materials, events, workflows, and learning paths. The portal is supported by multiple EU funding programs (Horizon 2020 & Horizon Europe) and the DAPHNE4NFDI consortium. It aims to facilitate training and knowledge sharing within the research community.

name: OERHub.at
description: OERhub.at is a search engine for Open Educational Resources (OER) from the Austrian higher education sector. It provides access to materials, workflows, learning paths, and events, specifically supporting the photon and neutron science community. The platform is supported by EU funding programs (Horizon 2020, Horizon Europe) and the DAPHNE4NFDI consortium, aiming to facilitate training in these scientific domains.

description: The PaN-Training platform provides access to resources for photon and neutron science, including events, materials, workflows, and learning paths. name: TeSShub
description: TeSShub is a platform for creating and hosting training catalogues. It allows communities to manage their own sub-portals with training content, enabling customized learning experiences. Spaces can be requested and are governed by administration teams, fostering a collaborative environment. Existing catalogues like pan-training and DALIA are hosted on TeSShub.
teaches: How to create and manage training catalogues
Utilizing spaces within the TeSS platform
Understanding the governance of TeSS spaces

description: RDMkit provides resources for Research Data Management (RDM) in Plant Science. It specifically addresses data planning for plant phenotyping and genotyping, supporting researchers in effectively managing their data lifecycle. This material links to the external RDMkit platform for practical application of these concepts. It’s a valuable resource for researchers and students in plant biology and related fields.
teaches: How to create a Data Management Plan for plant science research
Best practices for managing plant phenotyping data
Best practices for managing plant genotyping data

name: FAIR Cookbook - Plant genomic and genetic variation data submission to EMBL-EBI databases
description: This training material provides guidance on submitting plant genomic and genetic variation data to EMBL-EBI databases. It focuses on FAIR data principles and utilizes the FAIR Cookbook format. The material covers best practices for data publishing, adhering to standards like MIAPPE, and ensuring data reusability and interoperability. It's designed to help researchers effectively share their data with the community.
teaches: Submitting plant genomic data to EMBL-EBI databases
Applying FAIR data principles to plant genomics
Using MIAPPE standards for data annotation
